etymology.description: đ ¬ť(đ ¬ť) (fĂş, "to subdue") original pictogram -> ĺ‡ˇ(ĺ‡ˇ) (fĂˇn, "tray") added as semantic -> phono-semantic compound for 'to serve'
etymology.interpretation: The character đ ¬ť initially depicted a concept related to subduing or serving. It became part of a phono-semantic compound with the addition of ĺ‡ˇ(ĺ‡ˇ) to give the meaning of 'to serve'. This illustrates the evolution of its meaning through a combination of sound and related concepts.
